The user wants to rename a git branch from `ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select` to `ma-local-dev/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`. 

To rename a git branch, I need to:
1. If we're on the branch we want to rename: `git branch -m new-name`
2. If we're on a different branch: `git branch -m old-name new-name`

So the command would be:
`git branch -m ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select ma-local-dev/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`

This requires git_write permission since we're modifying git state.

The branch rename is already in effect. Your local branch list shows **`ma-local-dev/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`** (the old name `ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select` is no longer there). The first `git branch -m` did rename it; the only failure was updating the config file in the sandbox.

**Summary:** The local branch is now named `ma-local-dev/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`.

**If you use a remote:** The remote still has `origin/ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`. To use the new name on the remote you can:

1. Push the renamed branch:  
   `git push origin ma-local-dev/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`
2. Optionally remove the old remote branch:  
   `git push origin --delete ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select`
